messages.createManageCustomForms: Create and manage custom forms which best fit your use case. Custom Forms allow you to get details regarding a certain scenario when needed
messages.createManageCustomSubForms: Create and manage subforms which best fit your use case. Subforms act as forms within your forms allowing you to get additional details related to a certain scenario when needed. They can be set up to record single or multiple entries.
messages.setupForm: Create fields that best fit your business requirements, arrange the form to your liking, and include subforms if required. You are required to create fields for this form before you can arrange them. Once you have created the fields for this form and arranged it, you can then set the form status to active.
messages.setupSubform: Create fields that best fit your business requirements, arrange the subform to your liking, and include list views to your subform if required. You are required to create fields for this subform before you can arrange them. Once you have created the fields for this form and arranged it, you can then set the form status to active.

messages.multipleSubformRecords: Can record more than one subform

messages.chooseParentPicklistForDependency: Choose a parent picklist to define a dependency. This selection will link this picklist to a parent picklist, allowing you to map the parent picklist fields to the fields in this picklist within the mapping page. After mapping, selections made on a parent picklist within the form will then affect the options available for this picklist.
messages.selectParentAndChildOptions: Select a Parent Option and choose its Child Options. If you need to change the Parent Option, first deselect the current one and then select a new Parent Option. To remove any mapped fields, navigate to the Preview tab and clear the selections there.
messages.arrangeFormFields: Arrange the form to your liking by clicking on your desired field from the available fields box, then dragging and dropping it onto the canvas. You can rename the sections by clicking on the edit icons beside them. To remove a field or section, click on the 'x' button beside it.
messages.removeSectionAndFieldsWarning: This section and the fields tied to it will be removed from the Form builder. This cannot be undone.

messages.monthVariable: M characters represent the numeric month of the ticket creation date ('01' = Jan, '02' = Feb)
messages.dayVariable: D characters represent the numeric day of the month of the record creation date (E.g. '01' to '31' for Jan)

messages.importSimpleSteps: Our import function allows you to bring your data into FieldEx in just a few simple steps. Here's how:
messages.importSimpleStepsUpload: Upload your data: Drag and drop a .csv or .xls file, or browse your computer.
messages.importSimpleStepsMapping: Map your fields: Match the columns in your file to the corresponding fields in FieldEx.
messages.importSimpleStepsVerify: Verify and edit: Review your data, make any necessary changes, and remove rows if needed. The system will highlight any missing information in required fields to prevent import errors.
messages.importSimpleStepsImport: Import your data: Once everything looks good, click import to bring your data into FieldEx.
messages.importDataCsvFormat: To import your data into FieldEx,Please ensure the file you are uploading contains column headings and is of .csv format.
messages.exportDataAsCsvFormat: To export your data from FieldEx, click 'Export' and follow the steps. Your data will be downloaded as a .csv file and can be viewed as a spreadsheet.

sec24.original: Now, define what actions the workflow should take when triggered. You can choose to send notifications via email, display in-app messages, or perform other actions based on the conditions you've set. This step ensures that the right actions are taken automatically when the workflow runs.

This step ensures that the right actions are taken automatically when the workflow runs.

messages.helpAutomateInternal: Workflows let you set up automatic notifications based on specific conditions in any module you choose. You can customize notifications to be sent as in-app alerts or emails:
messages.inAppNotificationsDesc: Write custom messsages to notify FieldEx users directly in the web or mobile app when conditions are met.
messages.emailNotificationsDesc: Design custom email templates with field values to notify FieldEx users or external contacts.
messages.whetherRemindersCustomer: Whether it's reminders, customer updates, or team alerts, workflows make staying connected simple and effective.
messages.useThisPageToCreateNewWorkflows: Use this page to create new workflows or manage the ones you've already set up.
messages.triggerWhenNewRecordCreated: The workflow will trigger when a new record is added and the set conditions are met.
messages.triggerWhenUpdatedFirstTime: The workflow triggers the first time changes are made to the record and meet the specified conditions.
messages.triggerWhenRecordUpdatedEverytime: The workflow triggers every time changes are made to the record and meet the specified conditions.
messages.triggerWhenRecordDeleted: This option will trigger the workflow whenever a record is deleted.
messages.noConditionsAvailable: Click the 'Add/Edit Condition' button to set the rules for starting this workflow.
messages.noWorkflowActionsAvailable: No actions added yet. Click 'Add Notification' to create one.
messages.pleaseSelectOneTriggerEvent: Please select at least one trigger event
messages.haveNotCreateNotification: Haven't created a noification yet? Click the button below.
messages.forEmailNotification: For email notifications, create an email template
messages.chooseHowItCompares: Choose how it compares (e.g., 'is equal to')
messages.howItCompares: How it compares (e.g., 'is equal to' or 'is after')
messages.theDetailYoureLookingFor: The detail you're looking for (e.g., 'March 1st' or 'Completed')
messages.meansAllConditionsMustBeTrue: means all conditions must be true for the workflow to trigger
messages.egBothConditionsTrue: E.g.: If both 'Status is Complete' AND 'Date is March 1st' are true, then the workflow will start.
messages.egEitherConditionsTrue: E.g.: If either 'Status is Complete' OR 'Priority is High,' workflow will start.
messages.youCanGroupConditions: You can also group conditions together to create more complex rules, like combining AND and OR.
messages.youCanAlsoGroupConditions: You can also group conditions together to create more complex rules, like combining AND and OR.
messages.andOrConditionExample: '(A OR B) AND (C AND D AND E)' means:
messages.eitherAOrBMustBeTrue: Either A or B must be true,
messages.allCDEMustBeTrue: All of C, D, and E must also be true at the same time.
messages.orPartGivesFlexibility: The OR part gives you flexibility:
messages.onlyOneThingNeedsToHappen: Only one thing from that group needs to happen.
messages.everythingInGroupMustHappen: Everything in that group must happen.
messages.isGreaterThanOrEqualTo: is greater than or equal to
messages.isLessThanOrEqualTo: is less than or equal to

description.bin_under_stock: Sent to the managing group when one of their bins reaches or exceeds its under stock point
description.bin_re_order_point: Sent to the managing group when one of their bins reaches or exceeds its reorder point
description.bin_transfer_request: Sent to the approver when a bin transfer has been requested

description.job_order_assigned: Sent to a user when they have been assigned as order owner for a Job Order
description.job_order_completed: Sent to an order owner's manager when a Job Order status has changed to 'Completed' (or equivalent)
description.job_order_appointment_updated: Sent to the Job Order owner when its time and date has been updated through Calendly
description.survey_responded: Sent to the owner when a Job Order receives a Survey response from a Customer

description.maintenance_completed: Sent to the Equipment custodian when a Maintenance has been completed

description.task_assigned: Sent to a user when they have been assigned to a Task
description.task_location_updated: Sent to a user when the location of a Task they are assigned to has been updated
description.task_accepted: Sent to a user when someone has accepted a Task they assigned
description.task_rejected: Sent to the Task creator when a normal user (not owner) assigned to the Task rejects it
description.task_rejected_by_owner: Sent to the Task assignor when the Task owner rejects it
description.owner_task_completed: Sent to the assignor when a task's overall status has been marked as completed
description.user_task_completed: Sent to the task creator when an assigned individual has marked their Task as complete
description.task_deleted: Sent to the assignee of a Task when it is deleted
description.task_note_added: Sent to the Task assignor when a new note is added
description.task_note_added_for_task_members: Sent to task members when a new note is added

name.task_rejected_by_owner: Task has been Rejected by Owner
name.task_note_added: User has Added a Note to a Task
name.task_note_added_for_task_members: User has Added a Note to a Task

description.user_working_hour_changed: Sent to a user's manager when they change their working hours
description.user_added_to_team: Sent to a user when they have been added to a team
description.user_leave_application: Sent to a user's supervisor when they apply for leave
description.user_leave_accepted: Sent to a user when their leave request has been accepted
description.user_leave_reject: Sent to a user when their leave request has been rejected

name.user_working_hour_changed: User has Changed their Working Hours
name.user_added_to_team: You have been Added to a Team
